props.title: Open Source Lightweight React JSON Schema Form Library for MUI
props.description: Build dynamic, reusable forms from JSON schema with Material UI components — optimized for bundle size, tree-shaking, and developer experience.

props.title: Why Choose WorkflowEngine Core with MUI

features.title: MUI Components Pack
features.text: Production-ready form components built on top of MUI.

features.title: Lightweight Bundle
features.text: Only 188 KB gzip for Core + MUI (JS + CSS).

features.title: No Deep React Knowledge
features.text: Build complex forms without React expertise.

features.title: Single JSON Form
features.text: Entire form defined in one JSON file for structure + UI.

features.title: Reuse & Scale Across Projects
features.text: Build once, reuse everywhere with a single form engine and no dependency headaches.

features.title: Easy Customization, No CSS Pain
features.text: Easily adapt field sets and styling without deep CSS or UI work.

features.title: Schema-Driven Internationalization
features.text: Locale-based labels, placeholders, validation messages, and errors defined directly in the form schema.

features.title: MIT Licensed Core
features.text: Open-source core, safe for commercial use.

props.title: How WorkflowEngine Core fits into your stack
props.description: WorkflowEngine Core maps a JSON form definition to a dynamic HTML form and lets you plug in your own UI components — or use the ready-made MUI pack.

items.title: WorkflowEngine Core renders UI through MUI view

props.secondDescription: With WorkflowEngine, you get a comprehensive package of ready-to-use MUI components that can be easily customized to meet your specific needs.

props.title: Built for API-driven products and internal tools

faqItems.question: Is WorkflowEngine Core open source?
faqItems.answer: Yes. The core library is MIT-licensed, which is suitable for commercial use.

faqItems.question: How do I use it with MUI?
faqItems.answer: Install @react-form-builder/components-material-ui and render forms with FormViewer using the provided MUI view.

faqItems.question: Do I need a visual drag-and-drop builder?
faqItems.answer: No. You can render and manage forms purely from JSON. A visual builder can be added later if your workflow needs it.

faqItems.question: Can I store form schemas in a database and deliver them via API?
faqItems.answer: Yes — the JSON schema can be loaded from any source (file, DB, CMS, API) and rendered in the client.

faqItems.question: Does it work with Next.js / Remix?
faqItems.answer: Yes — it's designed to work smoothly with modern React stacks, including Next.js and Remix.

faqItems.question: What UI themes are supported?
faqItems.answer: MUI, shadcn/ui, React Suite, Mantine are listed as supported themes, with more coming soon.

faqItems.question: How small is the bundle?
faqItems.answer: The repository provides a benchmark example: ~188.54 KB gzip for WorkflowEngine Core (JS + CSS).

faqItems.question: Can I use my own MUI-based components?
faqItems.answer: Yes — the core is designed to render using your components; the MUI pack is a ready-made option.

props.title: Ready to build schema-driven MUI forms?
props.description: Start with the MIT-licensed core, render your first form, and keep your UI consistent with Material UI.
